mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals ongoing labor market expansion, which supports consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If month-over-month PAYEMS increases are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in fewer than 55% of instances over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 1.0%, further increases tighten financial conditions, raise discount rates applied to equity cash flows, and reduce equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If SPX 60-day returns following FEDFUNDS > 1.0% are positive in more than 50% of triggered quarters over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and decreases in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicate demand-pull expansion with tightening labor capacity, which boosts corporate pricing power and profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the joint behavior of RSAFS rising and UNRATE falling does not produce a walk-forward Rank IC >= 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of joint signal months.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, month-over-month increases in industrial production (INDPRO) predict negative S&P 500 5-day returns because higher output against rising policy rates signals margin compression from input costs and discount-rate effects that outweigh volume benefits.
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS is above 1.0%, positive month-over-month INDPRO changes are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in fewer than 55% of instances over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) below zero signals weakening consumer demand and falling corporate revenues, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If RSAFS monthly declines are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in fewer than 55% of instances over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in housing starts (HOUST) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal durable goods demand and construction activity strength, driving positive S&P 500 60-day returns through cyclical earnings expansion.
failure_condition: If the joint signal of HOUST and PAYEMS both increasing month-over-month does not produce positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of joint occurrences over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) above 0.2% signals an inflation surprise that elevates discount rate expectations and compresses real earnings, producing negative S&P 500 5-day forward returns.
regime_hint: low inflation transitioning to higher inflation
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL monthly increases greater than 0.2% are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in fewer than 55% of instances over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects aggregate demand expansion that translates into broad-based revenue growth for S&P 500 constituents, generating positive 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: If positive GDPC1 quarterly growth prints are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in fewer than 55% of instances over a rolling 5-year window.
